channel.title: Fire & Ice: An Iceland Road-Trip Podcast
channel.description: A family travel-and-learning podcast for the drives on a 10-day Iceland road trip. Two hosts, Anna and Magnus, tell the story of Iceland - volcanoes, Vikings, glaciers, elves, puffins and more - starting from zero and building day by day. Made for grown-ups and for kids aged 7 and 9 in the back seat.
channel.itunes:summary: A family travel-and-learning podcast for the drives on a 10-day Iceland road trip. Two hosts, Anna and Magnus, tell the story of Iceland - volcanoes, Vikings, glaciers, elves, puffins and more - starting from zero and building day by day. Made for grown-ups and for kids aged 7 and 9 in the back seat.
channel.itunes:author: The Zryumov family

item.title: Ep 1: The Land of Fire and Ice
item.itunes:title: The Land of Fire and Ice
item.description: What and where Iceland is: a brand-new island still being built on the Mid-Atlantic Ridge.
item.itunes:summary: What and where Iceland is: a brand-new island still being built on the Mid-Atlantic Ridge.

item.title: Ep 2: The Vikings Arrive
item.itunes:title: The Vikings Arrive
item.description: How an empty island got its people: Ingolfur Arnarson, the high-seat pillars, and the Althingi.
item.itunes:summary: How an empty island got its people: Ingolfur Arnarson, the high-seat pillars, and the Althingi.

item.title: Ep 3: Waterfalls and a Volcano You Can't Pronounce
item.itunes:title: Waterfalls and a Volcano You Can't Pronounce
item.description: Why Iceland has thousands of waterfalls, plus the 2010 Eyjafjallajokull eruption that grounded airplanes.
item.itunes:summary: Why Iceland has thousands of waterfalls, plus the 2010 Eyjafjallajokull eruption that grounded airplanes.

item.title: Ep 4: Glaciers and the Highlands
item.itunes:title: Glaciers and the Highlands
item.description: Rivers of ice, the valley of Thorsmork, and sudden glacial floods.
item.itunes:summary: Rivers of ice, the valley of Thorsmork, and sudden glacial floods.

item.title: Ep 5: Norse Gods and the Days of the Week
item.itunes:title: Norse Gods and the Days of the Week
item.description: Odin, Thor, Loki and Freyja - and why Thursday is literally Thor's day.
item.itunes:summary: Odin, Thor, Loki and Freyja - and why Thursday is literally Thor's day.

item.title: Ep 6: Turf Houses and How People Survived
item.itunes:title: Turf Houses and How People Survived
item.description: A thousand years of life in houses made of grass.
item.itunes:summary: A thousand years of life in houses made of grass.

item.title: Ep 7: Black Sand, Basalt Columns, and Stone Trolls
item.itunes:title: Black Sand, Basalt Columns, and Stone Trolls
item.description: Reynisfjara's geology and folklore - and its genuinely dangerous waves.
item.itunes:summary: Reynisfjara's geology and folklore - and its genuinely dangerous waves.

item.title: Ep 8: Puffins and the Birds of Iceland
item.itunes:title: Puffins and the Birds of Iceland
item.description: Sea parrots, pufflings, and the Arctic tern's pole-to-pole journey.
item.itunes:summary: Sea parrots, pufflings, and the Arctic tern's pole-to-pole journey.

item.title: Ep 9: The Laki Eruption of 1783
item.itunes:title: The Laki Eruption of 1783
item.description: The eruption that caused a famine and changed weather across the world.
item.itunes:summary: The eruption that caused a famine and changed weather across the world.

item.title: Ep 10: Carved by Rivers: Canyons, Moss, and Folklore
item.itunes:title: Carved by Rivers: Canyons, Moss, and Folklore
item.description: The Fjadrargljufur canyon and the legend of the sisters of Klaustur.
item.itunes:summary: The Fjadrargljufur canyon and the legend of the sisters of Klaustur.
